mnemonics.title: Memory Allocation Functions
mnemonics.content: M.A.R.C. - Malloc Allocates, Realloc Resizes, Calloc Clears (to zero). Always Remember to FREE!

mnemonics.title: malloc vs calloc
mnemonics.content: MALLOC = Memory ALLOCate (uninitialized, one argument). CALLOC = CLEAR ALLOCate (zero-initialized, two arguments).

mnemonics.title: NULL Check
mnemonics.content: Before using pointer from malloc/calloc/realloc: ALWAYS CHECK FOR NULL - Never Dereference a NULL!

mnemonics.title: realloc Rule
mnemonics.content: realloc pattern: SAVE TO TEMP, CHECK TEMP, THEN ASSIGN. Never: ptr = realloc(ptr, size);

acronyms.usage: In C, using malloc(), calloc(), realloc(), and free() functions to manage heap memory at runtime.
Allocates specified bytes of uninitialized memory from heap. Syntax: ptr = malloc(size);
Allocates memory for array and initializes to zero. Syntax: ptr = calloc(n, size);
Resizes previously allocated memory block. Syntax: ptr = realloc(ptr, newSize);

visualTips.visualization: Think of STACK as a stack of plates (LIFO, fixed size, automatic). Think of HEAP as a pile of scattered boxes (variable size, manual management).
Draw program memory as stacked segments: CODE at bottom, then STATIC/GLOBAL, then HEAP (grows up), then STACK (grows down). They meet in middle.
Imagine checking into hotel room but never checking out - the room stays occupied (memory leak). Each leak 'occupies' more hotel rooms until none left.
Like keeping address of friend's old house after they moved - the address still exists but points to nothing useful (or someone else lives there now).

analogies.analogy: Static memory is like wearing fixed-size clothes bought before knowing your size. Dynamic memory is like Tailor-Made clothing - you tell them your exact measurements and they create perfect fit.
Like reserving a hotel room - you get a specific room number (pointer) for your exclusive use until you check out (free).
Like returning a borrowed book to library - the book becomes available for others. If you don't return, library runs out of books (memory leak).
Like writing notes on sticky notes but never removing them from your desk - eventually no space remains to work.
